Show / Hide Table of Contents

Namespace AspNetCore.Multitenancy

Classes

InMemoryTenantStore

Simple in-memory implementation of ITenantStore. Use for unit tests, demos, and local development. Not suitable for production multi-instance deployments.

MultitenancyBuilder

Fluent builder returned by AddMultitenancy(IServiceCollection, Action<MultitenancyOptions>?). Use the With* extension methods from each package to register features.

MultitenancyOptions

Top-level configuration options for the multi-tenancy library. Configure via the AddMultitenancy(options => ...) builder.

ServiceCollectionExtensions

Extension methods for registering multi-tenancy services with the DI container and adding the tenant middleware to the pipeline.

TenantInfo

Represents a tenant in the system. All properties needed for routing, database isolation, and feature access live here.

TenantMiddleware

ASP.NET Core middleware that runs all registered ITenantResolvers in order and populates ITenantContext for the request.

TenantNotFoundException

Thrown when a tenant cannot be resolved from the current HTTP request, and the application requires a valid tenant context.

Interfaces

ITenantContext

Provides access to the current tenant context for the active request or execution scope. Inject this interface anywhere you need to know which tenant is in context.

ITenantResolver

Resolves the current tenant from an incoming HTTP request. Implement this interface to create a custom resolution strategy. Multiple resolvers can be registered; they are tried in order until one succeeds.

ITenantStore

Provides persistent storage and retrieval of TenantInfo records. Implement this to back tenants with a database, configuration file, or any other source.

Enums

IsolationStrategy

Specifies the database isolation strategy for tenant data.

TenantStore

Specifies where tenants are persisted.

In this article
